content.text:
```mdl
@ Contract: Confidentiality and Non‑Disclosure Agreement (Stockholder List Inspection)
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_14_nda_16

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
type PartyRole =
    | Company(unit)
    | Stockholder(unit)

type Party = {
    role: PartyRole,                 # which side of the Agreement the party is
    is_confidential_material_receiver: bool   # true for the Stockholder (the Receiving Party)
}

type ConfidentialMaterial = {
    is_confidential: bool,          # always true for the stockholder list and derived data
    destroyed: bool,                # true once the material has been destroyed
    returned: bool                  # true once the material has been returned to the Company
}

type Agreement = {
    terminated: bool,               # true when the Agreement has been terminated
    # (optional) termination_date could be added here if a temporal model of dates is needed
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – the public objects that will be aligned with other standards
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
entity party: Party
entity confidential_material: ConfidentialMaterial
entity agreement: Agreement

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – deontic requirements extracted from the Agreement
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – material material may be used only for purposes reasonably related to
#           the Stockholder’s interest as a stockholder.
rule O use_material_for_permitted_purposes:
    # The Receiving Party (the Stockholder) must not use the material for any
    # purpose other than the permitted one.  This is modelled as a prohibition
    # on any “other_use” flag that would be added to the ConfidentialMaterial
    # type; here we simply assert that the flag is always false.
    true always   # placeholder – the actual predicate would reference a field such as
                 # confidential_material.other_use, which is not present in the source.

@ Clause 2 – prohibition on disclosure, publication or communication
rule F disclose_confidential_material:
    # The Receiving Party must never disclose the material.
    # Since the model does not contain an explicit “disclosed” field,
    # we express the prohibition as a constant false requirement.
    false always

@ Clause 3 – notice requirement when disclosure is compelled by law
rule O notice_before_legal_disclosure:
    # When a legal demand occurs (not modelled explicitly), the Receiving Party
    # must give prompt notice to the Company.  This is expressed as an
    # obligation that the predicate “legal_demand” (a placeholder) implies
    # “notice_given” (also a placeholder).  Because the source does not
    # define these fields, the rule is left as a template.
    true always   # placeholder for the actual conditional obligation.

@ Clause 5 – remedies for breach (specific performance / injunctive relief)
rule O specific_performance_for_breach:
    # The Company is entitled to specific performance if the Stockholder breaches.
    # This is a rights‑based statement, not a constraint on the model, so we
    # encode it as a true obligation (always satisfied) to keep the model
    # syntactically complete.
    true always

@ Clause 6 – amendment / waiver only by written instrument
rule F amend_without_written_agreement:
    # No amendment may be made without a written instrument.
    false always   # placeholder – would be linked to a field “amended_by_written_agreement”.

@ Clause 8 – governing law (Delaware)
rule O governed_by_delaware_law:
    # The Agreement is governed by Delaware law.
    true always   # placeholder – no concrete field to check.

@ Clause 9 – execution in counterparts
rule O execution_in_counterparts:
    # The Agreement may be executed in multiple counterparts.
    true always   # placeholder – no concrete field to check.

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hypothesis – Receiving Party shall destroy or return Confidential Information
#              upon termination of the Agreement.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Hypothesis derived from the overall purpose of the NDA
rule O destroy_or_return_on_termination:
    # When the Agreement is terminated, the Receiving Party must either
    # destroy or return the Confidential Material.  The temporal pattern is:
    #   (agreement.terminated now) ⇒ (confidential_material.destroyed
    #                                      or confidential_material.returned) eventually
    (agreement.terminated now) implies
        (confidential_material.destroyed or confidential_material.returned) eventually

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# End of module
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
```
